paper_title: Palynology, Charcoal, and Principal Curve Analysis

methods.description: PrC analysis is a non-parametric statistical method used to identify gradients in ecological data. It starts with PCA or CA axis 1 scores and iterates using smoothing splines to increase fit to the data, capturing more variance than standard ordination techniques.

steps.description: Start with principal components analysis (PCA) or CA axis 1 scores as the starting curve.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize principal curve with PCA or CA axis 1 scores

steps.description: Iterate the starting curve using smoothing splines to increase fit to the data.
steps.searchable_summary: apply smoothing splines to iterate and fit the principal curve

methods.description: Construction of a Bayesian age-model using tephra age estimates, recalculated with the IntCal20 calibration curve, and incorporating prior age estimates for certain tephras.

steps.description: Recalculate tephra age estimates using the cross-referenced Bayesian age-model and update with the IntCal20 calibration curve.
steps.searchable_summary: recalculate tephra ages with Bayesian model and IntCal20 curve

steps.description: Input age of the Fosen Tephra with an arbitrary 1σ error range of 100 years.
steps.searchable_summary: input Fosen Tephra age with 100-year error range
outputs: Fosen Tephra age with error range

steps.description: Input age estimates for Borrobol and Penifiler tephras as 'priors' due to their non-normal distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: input Borrobol and Penifiler tephra ages as priors

steps.description: Use age-model extrapolation for the Tanera Tephra to the base of the QM1 sequence.
steps.searchable_summary: extrapolate Tanera Tephra age to QM1 sequence base
